titulo: IRC: Prolonged War Drives Mental Health Crisis Across Ukraine
contenido:
Country: Ukraine
Source: International Rescue Committee

Kyiv, Ukraine, April 29, 2026 — Data drawn from more than 5,000 psychological consultations provided by the International Rescue Committee (IRC) over the past year and into early 2026 reveals the devastating impact of prolonged conflict on mental health in Ukraine. Ukrainians’ ability to manage stress and carry out daily activities has deteriorated sharply, with most adults reporting symptoms of anxiety or depression.

  • An estimated 15 million people in Ukraine need psychological support today.
  • 60% of those seeking support are battling anxiety, and one in five is struggling with depression.
  • Around 80% of those in need are aged over 40, including many over 60 - groups with fewer mobility options and a heightened risk of isolation, chronic health problems, and loss.

The cumulative impact of the war, worsened by a particularly harsh winter Ukrainians have just endured and the lack of any real relief with the arrival of spring, continues to erode families’ ability to cope and manage daily life. Many people have been forced to cut back on essential needs such as medication, a trade‑off that directly increases psychological strain. Most individuals seek mental‑health support in
resumen_ollama: El IRC informa que el conflicto prolongado en Ucrania ha causado una crisis mental grave, con 15 millones de personas necesitando apoyo psicológico y un alto porcentaje sufriendo ansiedad o depresión. La situación se agrava con el invierno duro y la falta de alivio.

titulo: Myanmar: Taw Oo District Short Update: Burma Army shelling killed one villager, injured a pregnant woman, and caused damage in Daw Hpah Hkoh Township (December 2025 and February 2026) [EN/Karen/MY]
contenido:
Country: Myanmar
Source: Karen Human Rights Group

Please refer to the attached files.

This Short Update describes events occurring in Daw Hpah Hkoh (Thandaungyi) Township, Taw Oo (Toungoo) District, in December 2025 and February 2026. On December 27th 2025, the Burma Army cut off phone signal from Day Loh Mu Nu Lar Hta village tract and set up a checkpoint on a road within the same village tract. Later that night, they fired two shells into Aa--- place, Thandaungyi Section 1, Day Loh Mu Nu Lar Hta village tract. One of the shells injured a pregnant woman and also hit her house walls. Villagers believed that the shells were fired from the Karen New Year Hill in Thandaungyi Town. titulo: Afghanistan: IRC warns looming deportations risk Afghans returning to a country where one third of the population cannot feed their families
contenido:
Countries: Afghanistan, World
Source: International Rescue Committee

Meanwhile, Afghans still remain the largest group arriving in Europe through the Balkans

Kabul, Afghanistan, April 29, 2026 — As governments around the world begin to ramp up the deportation of Afghan refugees, the IRC is warning people would be returning to a country where 40% of the population is gripped by hunger, displacement caused by climate change and conflict continues, while women and girls face systemic barriers to education, employment, and even healthcare.

In a troubling shift, Afghans are now at risk of being deported from countries that took them in when they departed from Afghanistan. Under pressure from EU member states to crack down on irregular migration, Afghans are among those being sent back despite the humanitarian crisis they face at home. Afghans deported now would return to a country unable to guarantee their safety, feed them, or provide the healthcare and livelihoods they need to survive, and an uptick in new arrivals would only further stretch the limited resources available. As of late 2025, over 22,000 Afghans have been ordered to leave the EU.

Meanwhile, according to IRC’s data
resumen_ollama: El IRC advierte que las deportaciones de refugiados afghanos podrían llevar a una situación crítica, ya que el 40% de la población del país sufre hambre y enfrenta desplazamiento debido al cambio climático y a conflictos. Afghans son ahora los principales migrantes en Europa a través de los Balcanes.

titulo: EU Grants €45 Million Directly to Lebanon's Ministry of Social Affairs
contenido:
Country: Lebanon
Source: European Union

Please refer to the attached file.

The European Union and the Ministry of Social Affairs today announced new EU funding of €45 million. For the first time in many years, EU funding will be channelled directly through a Lebanese government institution. This marks a new phase in their partnership, working more closely with national institutions to deliver support to people across the country.

The announcement was made at the Grand Serail in the presence of Minister of Social Affairs Haneen Sayed, Minister of Finance Yassine Jaber, and EU Ambassador Sandra De Waele.

The EU funding includes €40 million for the AMAN programme — Lebanon's national cash assistance scheme — covering five months of payments for 90,000 of the most vulnerable Lebanese families, out of the 150,000 currently eligible. A further €5 million will support institutional reforms within the Ministry of Social Affairs, including updating the database of beneficiaries to make sure aid reaches the right people in the most efficient way.

Centcom has prepared a plan for a "short and powerful" wave of strikes against Iran that will likely target infrastructure, the article reported, citing three sources familiar with the matter.

The aim is that as a result, Iran will return to the negotiating table. 

Other options that could be discussed in the briefing include Washington seizing the Strait of Hormuz - with the possibility of a ground invasion - as well as a special forces operation to secure Iran’s stockpile of enriched uranium.

titulo: IRC launches emergency measles response for 20,000 children in Rohingya refugee camps as Bangladesh outbreak spreads
contenido:
Countries: Bangladesh, Myanmar
Source: International Rescue Committee

Dhaka, Bangladesh, April 29, 2026 — The International Rescue Committee (IRC) has launched an emergency vaccination and outbreak response in Rohingya refugee camps in Cox's Bazar as a widespread measles outbreak across Bangladesh reaches one of the world's most densely populated refugee camps.

Bangladesh is now battling one of its most serious measles outbreaks in decades, with the disease spreading to 58 of the country's 64 districts. As of 28 April, over 34,600 suspected cases have been reported and more than 200 people confirmed to have died from the disease. The risk of rapid transmission is acute in Cox's Bazar, where nearly a million Rohingya refugees live in overcrowded conditions with limited access to health services. The IRC is mobilizing swiftly to ensure children in the camps and surrounding host communities are reached before the outbreak accelerates further.

Hasina Rahman, IRC Bangladesh Director, said,

titulo: Israeli military chief says 'there is no ceasefire' with Lebanon
contenido:
Israeli military chief says 'there is no ceasefire' with Lebanon

The Israeli army Chief of Staff Eyal Zamir said that "there is no ceasefire" with Lebanon, as the military continues to launch demolitions and attacks in the country.

"In Lebanon, the mission assigned to us by the political echelon is to position ourselves along the line to prevent direct fire on the communities. We have achieved this; this is the line we are on. We may be required to remain on it," Zamir said during a visit to troops stationed in southern Lebanon. 

"On the combat front, there is no ceasefire; you continue to fight, to remove direct and indirect threats from the northern communities, to thwart terror infrastructure, to locate and kill terrorists," he added.

Since 2 March, Israeli forces have killed at least 2,290 people in Lebanon, and displaced around 1.2 million people nationwide.

titulo: Tanzania: The European Union provides €100,000 in support to people affected by devastating floods
contenido:
Country: United Republic of Tanzania
Source: European Commission's Directorate-General for European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations

Tuesday, 28 April 2026 – In late March 2026, intense and prolonged rainfall during the peak of the March–May rainy season triggered severe flooding and landslides in the Mbeya Region of Tanzania. In response, the European Union is providing €100,000 in humanitarian funding to support the most affected communities.

The disaster struck between the evening of 25 March and the early hours of 26 March, when continuous heavy rains and strong winds triggered multiple landslides and flash floods. The worst-affected wards—Nkunga, Lupepo, Kawetele and Ikuti in Rungwe District—saw homes buried and critical infrastructure, including roads, severely damaged.

Around 600 displaced people are currently sheltering in schools and temporary facilities, where overcrowding, limited access to safe drinking water and inadequate sanitation pose serious health risks. As of 27 March, at least 20 people—many of them children—had lost their lives, and several others were injured.

titulo: Democratic Republic of the Congo - Eastern region: Overview of incidents against humanitarian workers (March 2026)
contenido:
Country: Democratic Republic of the Congo
Source: UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs

Please refer to the attached Infographic.

The number of incidents affecting humanitarian actors in eastern DRC fell from 46 in February 2026 to 33 in March, a decrease of 28.3 per cent. Of these 33 incidents, 11 (33%) occurred in North Kivu, nine (27%) in South Kivu, nine (27%) in Tanganyika and four (12%) in Ituri, while no incident was reported in Maniema. Since January, four humanitarian workers were killed (three in Nord Kivu and one in Ituri) with three of these deaths occuring in March and the other one in February. In addition, one was injured in North Kivu.

Since January 2025, a total of 742 incidents affecting humanitarian actors were reported. North Kivu remained the most affected province with 334 incidents (45% of the total), followed by South Kivu with 215 (29%), Ituri with 100 (13%), Tanganyika with 71 (10%), and Maniema with 22 (3%). This distribution confirms persistent insecurity across eastern DRC, with North Kivu and South Kivu continuing to account for the majority

titulo: Syria Protection Sector: Protection Monitoring - Summary of Key Findings, Quarterly Report (January - March 2026)
contenido:
Country: Syrian Arab Republic
Sources: Protection Cluster, UN High Commissioner for Refugees

Please refer to the attached file.

1. Introduction

Between January and March 2026, the Syria Protection Sector continued its coordinated, country-wide protection monitoring exercise at community level through its network of partners, using the harmonized Key Informant Interview (KII) tool. The exercise aims to track protection risks, identify emerging patterns, and inform evidencebased programming, advocacy, and response planning.

This report presents an analytical overview of findings from the January to March 2026 round, focusing on the prevalence, severity, and interlinkages of protection risks across Syria.

titulo: South Sudan Joint Market Monitoring Initiative (JMMI) 1 - 13 April 2026
contenido:
Country: South Sudan
Source: REACH Initiative

Please refer to the attached file.

KEY FINDINGS

• Between March and April 2026, the median cost of the Multi-Sector Survival Minimum Expenditure Basket (MSSMEB) increased by 17%, while the food basket rose by 19%. This increase was mainly driven by higher prices of key commodities, including maize grain (50%), soap (29%), sorghum grain (26%), beans (26%), and charcoal (6%).

• In April 2026, the highest MSSMEB prices were recorded in Ajuong Thok Refugee Camp (Pariang County), Bentiu IDP Camp (Rubkona County), Mingkaman (Awerial County), Malakal Town (Malakal County), Pariang Town (Pariang County), Jamjang ( Pariang County), and Renk Town (Renk County).

• Overall, MSSMEB and food basket costs increased across several locations during this period, placing additional pressure on household purchasing power, largely driven by rising prices of essential items such as cereals, soap, beans, and charcoal, as well as broader constraints including high taxation (38%), currency depreciation (34%), elevated fuel prices (34%),
